title: The Butterjunk Effect

jokes.text: Oh, oh, it's always so hard to decide. Um, uh, tap. I mean bottled.
jokes.setup: Waiter asks simple tap vs bottled water question
jokes.punchline: Kif finds this trivial decision agonizingly difficult and changes his mind mid-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor - Kif's indecisiveness is well-established, and the trivial nature of the choice makes his anxiety absurd

jokes.text: Ooh, I love your boots, Leela. Nobody would ever guess they're knockoffs. How could you tell? I guessed.
jokes.setup: Amy compliments Leela's boots saying no one would guess they're knockoffs
jokes.punchline: When asked how she could tell, Amy admits she guessed - revealing she actually could tell
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - the 'compliment' immediately undercuts itself with perfect logical contradiction

jokes.text: 'Bam!' followed by compliment about knockoff boots
jokes.setup: Amy exclaims 'Bam!' suggesting confidence
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals she's complimenting obviously fake boots
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline structure with the confident 'Bam!' immediately undercut by the backhanded compliment about knockoffs.

jokes.text: Like how I might say I like Amy's dress 'cause it covers all her tramp stamps.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining how girlfriends talk trash to each other
jokes.punchline: Her example is a backhanded compliment about Amy's dress covering tattoos
jokes.explanation: Good example of the 'compliment that's actually an insult' trope with specific details

jokes.text: Now, enough about who is and who isn't a Chinese skank.
jokes.setup: Previous exchange about girlfriends trash-talking
jokes.punchline: Leela's attempt to end the bickering by summarizing it in the most blunt, offensive way possible
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that's funny through its inappropriate directness, though relies somewhat on offensive language

jokes.text: Oh, those lunar crybabies claim the rocks were stolen by the Apollo astronauts. They consider them part of their cultural heritage.
jokes.setup: Fry asking why they're bringing moon rocks to the moon
jokes.punchline: The moon inhabitants want their own rocks back, treating it as cultural theft
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on cultural appropriation and historical artifacts, with good sci-fi twist

jokes.text: 'Why are we bringing moon rocks to the moon?'
jokes.setup: Crew delivering moon rocks to the moon
jokes.punchline: Fry points out the obvious logical absurdity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pointing out of absurd logic. Fry's role as the 'why are we doing this?' character works well here.

jokes.text: Nice work, Leela. You're really pulling your weight. Which is saying something.
jokes.setup: Bender compliments Leela on her work
jokes.punchline: The compliment becomes an insult about her weight
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - starts nice then turns mean. Structure works but the fat joke is predictable

jokes.text: You bought that cultural heritage hoo-ha? What a bunch of big-city dummies.
jokes.setup: Moon inhabitants wanting their rocks back for cultural reasons
jokes.punchline: Moon person dismissing cultural heritage as nonsense while calling Earth people 'big-city dummies'
jokes.explanation: Good irony - moon person calls space travelers 'big-city' and dismisses his own people's claims

jokes.text: I don't even need my over-the-shoulder Boulder holder.
jokes.setup: Talking about how light the rocks are on the moon
jokes.punchline: Boulder holder = bra joke, playing on 'over-the-shoulder boulder holder' phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic pun structure but fairly predictable - the 'boulder holder' joke is well-known

jokes.text: It's like being a voice actor.
jokes.setup: Explaining how you can work and be lazy at the same time with low gravity
jokes.punchline: Comparison to voice acting - meta joke about the show itself being animated
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor - animated character commenting on voice acting. Perfect economy and self-awareness

jokes.text: Wait, the butterfly derby is when? And what?
jokes.setup: Moon person mentions butterfly derby casually
jokes.punchline: Fry's confused double-take at this bizarre concept
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy - Fry as audience surrogate confused by weird sci-fi concept

jokes.text: It's a necessity of the sport, it is.
jokes.setup: Explaining why butterfly derby players wear skimpy outfits
jokes.punchline: Obvious excuse for sexualized sport delivered with fake sincerity
jokes.explanation: Classic 'transparent excuse' humor with hillbilly character voice adding charm

jokes.text: This is what I was expecting.
jokes.setup: Bender previously said 'It's not what I was expecting' when seeing elegant sport
jokes.punchline: Now seeing violent team name 'Murderflies', he deadpans this was expected
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure with great timing and Bender's cynical worldview paying off

jokes.text: You see that there? That's called a floor. Once a butterfly hits that, she's out.
jokes.setup: Explaining rules of butterfly derby sport
jokes.punchline: Extremely obvious explanation delivered as if profound - 'that's called a floor'
jokes.explanation: Excellent condescending explanation of the obvious, perfectly delivered in character

jokes.text: Keep your eye on the ground. That's where she's going to land. Yep, called it.
jokes.setup: Predicts where butterfly will land - on the ground
jokes.punchline: Acts proud of predicting something inevitable
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the 'floor' joke - character taking credit for obvious prediction

jokes.text: Judging from your tank top, you love getting hooted at by hillbillies.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting Leela should volunteer for the contest
jokes.punchline: Insulting assumption about Leela's clothing choices and sexuality
jokes.explanation: Decent insult with specific details, fits Amy's character but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: And you could use the $50 for another gallon of hello kitty perfume.
jokes.setup: Leela firing back at Amy
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd image of Amy buying perfume by the gallon in childish scent
jokes.explanation: Good comeback with specific absurd details - 'gallon' and 'Hello Kitty' make it funny

jokes.text: The bicker sisters teaming up? Talk about cat women on the moon.
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela agreeing to team up despite fighting
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Cat Women on the Moon' (B-movie) and their catty behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever B-movie reference that works on multiple levels - literally on moon and 'catty' behavior

jokes.text: Why don't you and I ever do things together?
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela just agreed to team up for violent sport
jokes.punchline: Amy acts like they never spend time together despite being coworkers/friends
jokes.explanation: Mild irony humor - characters who are always together acting like strangers

jokes.text: This is hard. Now I know why butterflies are always so grouchy.
jokes.setup: Amy struggling with butterfly derby equipment/movement
jokes.punchline: False premise that butterflies are grouchy, delivered as revelation
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - butterflies aren't grouchy but Amy treats this as obvious fact

jokes.text: And we failed because we accidentally kicked those two rockettes to death?
jokes.setup: Leela reminiscing about trying out for Rockettes with Amy
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of accidentally killing people during audition
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation - starts innocent then casually mentions accidental murder. Perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Yay. They can move their arms.
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela just got beaten badly in their first match
jokes.punchline: Bender's extremely low bar for celebrating their 'success'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender deadpan - celebrating the absolute minimum achievement with fake enthusiasm

jokes.text: Nothing that doesn't happen to everyone eventually.
jokes.setup: Asked what happened to the last big thing in butterfly derby
jokes.punchline: Ominous non-answer implying death while making it sound inevitable
jokes.explanation: Cleverly evasive answer that's technically true but deeply unsettling. Great economy

jokes.text: What is this, an inquisition? You in or not?
jokes.setup: Reasonable follow-up questions about the sport
jokes.punchline: Treating basic questions as unreasonable interrogation
jokes.explanation: Good shady promoter character moment - deflecting reasonable questions aggressively

jokes.text: But just when it looked like they'd never win... They didn't.
jokes.setup: Classic sports movie narrator setup implying a turnaround
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by confirming they continued losing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Subverts sports movie tropes brilliantly

jokes.text: 'And although sometimes they got beaten, other times, they got beaten badly.'
jokes.setup: Montage of their fighting career struggles
jokes.punchline: False escalation - 'badly' isn't actually worse than just 'beaten'
jokes.explanation: Clever false escalation that sounds like it's building to something worse but really isn't. The structure works well for the montage format.

jokes.text: Even that team that turned out to be us in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Amy listing all the teams they lost to
jokes.punchline: They were so bad they lost to their own reflection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation of their failure - losing to a mirror is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Whoa, that puts the lead in my pencil.
jokes.setup: Leela tries the nectar supplement
jokes.punchline: Uses masculine sexual euphemism inappropriately for female character
jokes.explanation: Mild gender role reversal humor - woman using male sexual euphemism shows effect of drug

jokes.text: Or... Inject it between your toes. Either way works, right?
jokes.setup: Discussing how to take the 'supplement'
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of injection method like hardcore drug use
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing this 'supplement' is clearly drugs, delivered casually

jokes.text: So are carrots, but you don't see me injecting them between my toes.
jokes.setup: Leela argues nectar is natural
jokes.punchline: Professor's absurd reductio ad absurdum about injecting vegetables
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and logical absurdity - natural doesn't mean safe for injection

jokes.text: Sorry, Fry. I already got a double date with arm and hammer.
jokes.setup: Fry asks Leela on a date
jokes.punchline: She's too busy working out - 'arm and hammer' as exercise partners
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'Arm & Hammer' brand and literal arm muscles, fits steroid storyline

jokes.text: Oh, so now I'm your last-resort Booty call, huh? Okay, let's go.
jokes.setup: Fry asks Bender to hang out after being rejected by Leela
jokes.punchline: Bender correctly identifies he's second choice but agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - self-aware about being second choice but too lazy to care

jokes.text: Cork your face bladder. I'm working!
jokes.setup: Kif trying to be romantic during Amy's workout
jokes.punchline: Amy's crude dismissal calling his mouth a 'face bladder'
jokes.explanation: Creative insult showing Amy's steroid rage - 'face bladder' is memorably gross

jokes.text: Oh, your son's going to die all right... But from neglect. 'Cause you're going home in an urn. A funerary urn.
jokes.setup: Opponent mentions her dying son wants to see her win
jokes.punchline: Amy's escalating threats that get increasingly specific and dark
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from child neglect to death threats, with Amy clarifying 'funerary' urn showing roid rage

jokes.text: She just wants to hit me with various chairs.
jokes.setup: Kif complaining Amy doesn't want to cuddle
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about furniture-based violence showing extent of problem
jokes.explanation: Good specific absurd detail - 'various chairs' suggests she's tried multiple furniture types

jokes.text: You're lucky. I can't even get Leela to verbally abuse me.
jokes.setup: Kif complaining about being physically abused
jokes.punchline: Fry envies this, wishes Leela would at least acknowledge him through abuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - his pathetic romantic standards make abuse seem desirable

jokes.text: Wow! It's the second most beautiful thing I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Fry sees beautiful alien landscape
jokes.punchline: Only rates it second, implying something else is more beautiful
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation that he'll reveal what's most beautiful, good character setup

jokes.text: Hey Leela, want to know what the most beautiful thing is? No. Let's get hopped up.
jokes.setup: Fry offers to share romantic thought with Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately shuts him down, focused only on drugs
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to previous setup, shows Leela's drug obsession overriding romance

jokes.text: Suck 'em, burn 'em... as long as they're not butterflies, I don't give a grand crap.
jokes.setup: Park ranger explaining environmental rules
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to everything except butterflies, crude language
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - park ranger who only cares about one specific animal

jokes.text: What's he gonna do, migrate at us?
jokes.setup: Warning about aggressive male butterfly
jokes.punchline: Amy dismisses threat using butterfly behavior as verb
jokes.explanation: Creative use of 'migrate' as a threat - good wordplay on butterfly behavior

jokes.text: But believe me, you'll rue the day you came to the butterfly preserve.
jokes.setup: Vague warnings about unspecified dangers
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic threat about visiting a butterfly preserve
jokes.explanation: Dramatic overstatement for a nature preserve visit - sets up future consequences

jokes.text: I don't even know why we're helping find an addictive drug for our mean girlfriends.
jokes.setup: The guys helping their girlfriends find nectar
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt assessment of the obviously bad situation they're enabling
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of clarity - Fry stating the obvious absurdity of their situation

jokes.text: Look, the hammer's already out; I got to smash something.
jokes.setup: Bender pulls out hammer to attack butterfly despite warnings
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic that having weapon out requires using it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - destructive impulse justified by having weapon available

jokes.text: It smells like a skunk stuffed with feta cheese.
jokes.setup: Butterfly releases terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Specific grotesque imagery combining two awful smells
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out imagery - combining two distinctly awful smells for compound effect

jokes.text: Thank God I don't have a nose.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting to terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Bender grateful for his robot anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor using Bender's robot nature as advantage

jokes.text: That's a day at the beach compared to this.
jokes.setup: Fry mentions flies eat excrement, Kif's dad being a fly
jokes.punchline: Even fly who eats poop thinks butterfly stench is worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - creature that literally eats excrement finds something worse

jokes.text: Keep your door unlocked tonight. But McGruff the crime dog says... oh!
jokes.setup: Leela tells Fry to keep door unlocked
jokes.punchline: Fry starts citing crime prevention PSA before realizing the implication
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Fry's innocent nature vs. sudden realization about sex

jokes.text: I'm done polishing my ass, Fry. Here's your toothbrush back.
jokes.setup: Bender emerging from bathroom while Fry and Leela are in bed
jokes.punchline: Reveals he used Fry's toothbrush for inappropriate cleaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - completely inappropriate and selfish behavior delivered casually

jokes.text: 'As God is my witness, I'll never forgive you for this!'
jokes.setup: Finding Amy with Fry
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top dramatic declaration referencing Gone with the Wind
jokes.explanation: Classic melodramatic movie reference that fits the over-the-top jealousy situation. Recognizable quote adds impact.

jokes.text: This planet's got it going on.
jokes.setup: Chaos of love triangle fight between Amy, Leela, and Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual approval of the dramatic situation
jokes.explanation: Good Bender reaction - enjoying others' romantic drama with typical detachment

jokes.text: Sweet orgy of Georgia!
jokes.setup: Professor discovering the love triangle situation
jokes.punchline: Mangled exclamation combining 'Sweet Georgia' with 'orgy'
jokes.explanation: Good Professor exclamation - typical mangled phrase with sexual undertones

jokes.text: 'Sweet orgy of Georgia! Have you girls gone wild?'
jokes.setup: Professor discovering the three-way situation
jokes.punchline: Creative profanity combining 'Sweet Georgia' with orgy reference
jokes.explanation: 'Sweet orgy of Georgia' is wonderfully creative profanity that sounds like the Professor. The 'Girls Gone Wild' reference adds contemporary relevance.

jokes.text: Maybe they could just go warm Turkey?
jokes.setup: Professor says they must quit 'cold turkey'
jokes.punchline: Kif's gentle suggestion to modify the harsh approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif moment - trying to find gentler alternative to harsh treatment

jokes.text: 'I'm not surprised. Those pheromones were highly concentrated.' 'Oh, I think I'm getting a little... caterpilly.'
jokes.setup: Professor explaining pheromone effects
jokes.punchline: Amy's made-up word for transformation feeling
jokes.explanation: 'Caterpilly' is a perfect Amy-ism - she invents a cutesy word for a horrifying transformation. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, I think I'm getting a little... caterpilly.
jokes.setup: Leela feeling withdrawal effects from nectar
jokes.punchline: Made-up word describing transformation into caterpillar form
jokes.explanation: Creative made-up word that's both cute and disturbing for the transformation scene

jokes.text: Amy, if we get killed, I just want to say it's been an honor riding the roller coaster of addiction and recovery with you, as a team.
jokes.setup: Facing possible death in lava match
jokes.punchline: Heartfelt speech about their drug addiction journey
jokes.explanation: Great earnest absurdity - treats addiction like a team-building exercise with genuine emotion

jokes.text: I once killed a sea turtle with these boots.
jokes.setup: Amy asking how opponent isn't being hurt by lava
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of killing innocent sea life with footwear
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - casual mention of environmental crime explains toughness

jokes.text: 'He's even more beautiful than before!'
jokes.setup: Fry transformed into giant butterfly
jokes.punchline: Leela finding the horrific transformation attractive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how the pheromones have completely altered Leela's perception. The transformation being 'more beautiful' is absurdly romantic.

jokes.text: 'I am so sorry for allowing this lurid sex act to taint an otherwise delightful day of family-friendly violence.'
jokes.setup: Public mating display during sports event
jokes.punchline: Apology that highlights the absurd moral priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of moral priorities where violence is family-friendly but sex is scandalous. The formal apology format makes the absurdity more pointed. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That is some freaky bug-on-bug action. I feel a little left out.
jokes.setup: Fry as giant butterfly mating with opponents
jokes.punchline: Bender's inappropriate interest in the disturbing scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - inappropriate sexual interest in bizarre situation

jokes.text: 'That is some freaky bug-on-bug action. I feel a little left out.'
jokes.setup: Watching Fry mate with butterfly opponents
jokes.punchline: Amy's jealousy over missing the group mating
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Amy response to the situation. Her feeling 'left out' of the interspecies orgy is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: He's grown all these strange insect parts, and he has absolutely no brain activity. Look! He lost the strange insect parts. He's back to normal!
jokes.setup: Professor examining transformed Fry, noting lack of brain activity
jokes.punchline: When insect parts disappear, Professor declares him normal despite brain comment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant implication that Fry normally has no brain activity - perfect character assassination disguised as medical update

tmdb_overview: Leela and Amy become addicted to the performance enhancing drug known as "Nectar" to increase their chances of winning at the epic sport of Butterfly Derby.
